text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: The chief said that it is not every year that the spirit of Naamwin manifests itself, and when it does, the diviners are the first to know and the ordinary people are the last to know.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When the meat is not well cooked, if you put salt on it, it will not be as tasty as when the meat is well cooked, and the salt will rather make it even more tasteless.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: When a hunter kills an animal and brings it to his house, they do not eat it there but rather prepare it outside

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: “Go tell my son that I have sent you to Pharaoh to bring out the Israelites.”

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: When the man has done all these things, he will then take the fowl and slaughter it, and he will give the fowl's blood to the man to drink.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: The chiefs then said that it is Nãaŋmɩn who spoils the 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ and the nɩtorsɩ so that when they sow, they don't get good produce, and that if they make sacrifices to him, they will get good produce.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When the meat is not well cooked, and they have not added enough salt so it is not tasty, the person whose meat it is will be annoyed with those who have not done it well.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: When a hunter kills an animal and is bringing it to his house, they sing and dance with joy on the way

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: “Go and tell my servant David that I will make you shepherd over my people Israel.”

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: When you have finished doing your work, you will come and eat, and you will give me food to eat with my husband's work.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.

translated: The chief said that it is the Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ festival that makes the young men and women dance, and it is on it that they prepare porridge and food to eat, and when they have finished eating the porridge and food they will dance again.

translated: When a man has a farm with yams, if they do not have yams, it is not that the man does not have a good farm, it is that the yams have not produced many tubers for him to be happy.

text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”

translated: The chief said that it is the Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ elders who are to perform the funeral rites and the nɩtorsɩ elders are to perform the burial rites, and that whoever does not perform the funeral rites properly will be cursed, and whoever does not perform the burial rites properly will be cursed too.

translated: When the meat is not well cooked, and they have not added salt to it so that it is not well cooked, the meat is not properly cooked and the sauce is not tasty either, they will add salt to the sauce.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.

translated: When the man gave him the money and the cowry shells, he gave him a sheep too, and he gave him a guinea fowl to accompany the man to his house.

text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When a man has a farm with yams, if they don't have a good harvest the man and his farm are not to blame but the man and his hoe are at fault.

text: Yesu na wa pãa duoro a Jerusalɛm, wʊ tara na a poturbo pie nɩ ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba,
translated: Jesus and his disciples were on their way to Jerusalem. He was leading the way, and they were following him. They were on the road to a village called Samaria.

text: Wʊ dɔɔl ban a wʊ nuur sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn kʊ ba, tɩ chen.

translated: If you have a bad heart, you think that it is your companion who has wronged you, but you don't think about your own bad heart. You think your companion's heart is bad, but have you examined your own heart, you think you are good but your heart is bad, what will happen to you?
